prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: I believe the surrealist writer and painter Leonora Carrington is tragically under-discussed as a literary figure. While celebrated for her visual art, most people miss that her written work is just as visionary, creating entire mythologies from a blend of folklore, alchemy, and personal symbolism. Her stories aren't just surreal exercises; they are darkly funny, feminist fairy tales that operate on their own impeccable, dream-logic. To read her is to enter a completely unique literary universe that has no real parallel, one that prioritizes the intuitive and the magical over conventional narrative.
